items.title: Elevance sues CMS after Medicare Advantage stars recalculation
items.reason: This reports a lawsuit filed by Elevance (a major payer) against CMS involving Medicare Advantage star ratings and a $115 million financial impact—a direct regulatory enforcement matter involving a named payer.
items.summary: Elevance Health has sued CMS after the agency recalculated Medicare Advantage quality scores following a lawsuit loss to Clover Health. Elevance alleges that CMS applied a different recalculation methodology to Clover than to other plans like itself, causing Elevance to lose an estimated $115 million in star-rating-based revenues. The recalculation occurred last month and directly impacts how payers' quality performance is scored and reimbursed under Medicare Advantage. This litigation is significant for revenue-cycle teams because star rating outcomes drive quality-bonus payments and plan competitiveness in MA markets.
